 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes'). Functionality complete: ntm modes list with --category, --tier, --all, --format flags; ntm modes explain for mode details. All acceptance criteria met.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28o9m","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28v5n","title":"Task: Implement token budget advisor","description":"## Implementation
Add token estimation + budget advice:

1. Estimate per-mode cost using token heuristics + calibration data.
2. `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total.
3. `--budget` warns if estimate exceeds limit.
4. Suggest alternative modes based on value/cost ratio.

## Logging
- Log estimates + calibration deltas + budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + warning thresholds
- Integration: budget warning on sample config
- E2E: estimate output + warning path

## Dependencies
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Budget tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:20:03.442522261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:11:26.294355492Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:11:26.294099229Z","close_reason":"Completed (already implemented; verified + tests)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-1zs9y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-28v5n","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-297mu","title":"Task: Add robot preflight command using DCG","description":"## Overview
Expose a robot-first command to preflight an arbitrary shell command via DCG.

## Proposed Interface
- `ntm --robot-guard --cmd "rm -rf /tmp"`
- Output: {success, allowed, reason, dcg_version}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns structured JSON with allow/deny + rationale
- Fails gracefully if dcg missing
- No command execution; preflight only","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29dh","title":"Task: Implement one-click replay","description":"## Purpose
Enable replaying a prompt to a new or existing agent with one action.

## Scope
- Add replay action to history TUI (Enter or 'r' key)
- Options: replay to new agent, replay to specific agent, replay with modifications
- Track replay lineage for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can replay any historical prompt
- Replay spawns new agent or sends to existing
- Can edit prompt before replay
- Replay history tracks parent prompt

## Technical Notes
- Reuse spawn/send logic from existing commands
- Consider prompt templates for common modifications","notes":"Assume existing history storage; focus on UI/UX replay flow only.

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-29kr","title":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse","description":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse.

## Problem Being Solved

Users frequently spawn sessions with same configuration. Profiles allow saving and reusing spawn configs.

## Command Design

### Save Profile
```bash
ntm profile save PROFILE_NAME [--from SESSION]
ntm profile save myproject --cc=3 --cod=2 --prompt-file init.md
```

### Use Profile
```bash
ntm spawn SESSION --profile PROFILE_NAME
ntm spawn daily_work --profile myproject
```

### List Profiles
```bash
ntm profile list
```

### Delete Profile
```bash
ntm profile delete PROFILE_NAME
```

## Profile Storage
~/.config/ntm/profiles/PROFILE_NAME.toml

```toml
[spawn]
cc = 3
cod = 2
gmi = 0

[prompts]
init_prompt_file = "init.md"

[options]
auto_accept = true
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-profile-list
ntm --robot-profile-show=PROFILE_NAME

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm profile save creates profile file in ~/.config/ntm/profiles/
2. ntm profile list shows all saved profiles
3. ntm profile delete removes profile
4. ntm spawn --profile loads profile and uses config
5. Profile config overridden by explicit flags
6. Robot mode returns JSON for list/show
7. Profile file uses TOML format

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/profile_test.go
- Test save/load/delete operations
- Test profile merging with flags

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PROFILES] Session Profiles Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PROFILES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing session profiles"
ntm profile save test_profile --cc=2 --cod=1
ntm profile list | grep test_profile || exit 1
ntm profile delete test_profile
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Profiles test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session profiles save spawn config
2. Profiles include agent counts and prompts
3. Named profiles stored in config directory
4. ntm spawn --profile NAME loads profile
5. ntm profile list shows available profiles
6. ntm profile save NAME creates profile
7. Unit tests for profile serialization
8. E2E test for profile creation and loading","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
